lines.text: The shoot wrapped two hours ago. He's still here, developing film the old way. You stayed without being asked.

lines.speaker: Dex
lines.text: Can I tell you something weird?

lines.speaker: Dex
lines.text: I grew up in a town where nobody looked like me. So I started taking pictures. Proof that we existed, you know?
He shows you a photo from his phone. A kid in a backyard. Squinting into the sun. It's him.

lines.speaker: Dex
lines.text: I found the eighth frame. You know — the one you never take because you think the moment's already gone?
Something unlocks. A note. A frame. A small piece of who he actually is.
